description: Migration: schedule table no longer enforces one record per workflow_id
Migration: existing schedule rows survive the many-per-workflow migration with intact runner state
DB: schedule create persists required name
DB: schedule create rejects empty name
DB: schedule create persists payload_json
DB: schedule update persists modified payload_json
Action: global list returns schedules across workflows for the tenant
Action: get schedule returns schedule payload and timing data for editing
Action: create schedule succeeds for a published workflow with pinned payload schema and valid one-time payload
Action: create schedule succeeds for a published workflow with pinned payload schema and valid recurring payload
Action: edit schedule updates runAt and payload for a one-time schedule
Action: edit schedule updates cron, timezone, and payload for a recurring schedule
Action: pause schedule clears or disables runner registration without deleting the record
Action: resume schedule re-registers the runner using current schedule payload and timing
Action: delete schedule removes the schedule record and associated runner registration
Guard: create schedule fails when the workflow has no published version
Guard: create schedule fails when the workflow payload schema mode is inferred
Guard: edit schedule fails when the target workflow is not schedulable due to inferred schema mode
Validation: create schedule returns schema issues for invalid payload_json
Validation: edit schedule returns schema issues for invalid payload_json
Publish lifecycle: valid schedules rebind to the newest published workflow version
Publish lifecycle: invalid schedules are preserved and marked non-runnable when the new published version rejects their saved payload
Publish lifecycle: revalidation runs for every schedule attached to the workflow, not just one schedule
Publish lifecycle: a workflow with mixed valid and invalid schedules only rebinds the valid ones
Runner: one-time schedule launch passes schedule payload_json as workflow input_json
Runner: recurring schedule launch passes schedule payload_json as workflow input_json
Runner: schedule launch still stores schedule provenance metadata on the workflow run
Runner: invalid saved payload at fire time does not start execution and records schedule error state
UI: Automation Hub shows a Schedules tab and route state
UI: schedules list shows schedule name, workflow, trigger type, timing, status, and error columns
UI: schedules list filters by workflow
UI: schedules list filters by trigger type
UI: schedules list filters by status
UI: schedules list text search matches schedule name and workflow name
UI: schedules list row action opens edit dialog with current values
UI: schedules list row action pauses a currently enabled schedule
UI: schedules list row action resumes a paused schedule
UI: schedules list row action deletes a schedule after confirmation
UI: create dialog requires workflow selection, schedule name, and trigger type before save
UI: create dialog shows runAt input for one-time schedules
UI: create dialog shows cron and timezone inputs for recurring schedules
UI: form mode renders schema-driven fields for payload editing
UI: JSON mode allows raw payload editing for schedules
UI: invalid payload in form mode blocks save and shows field-level issues
UI: invalid payload in JSON mode blocks save and shows schema issues
UI: schedules cannot be created for inferred-schema workflows and the dialog explains why
Workflow editor: trigger selector only shows No trigger and Event
Workflow editor: one-time and recurring inline trigger panels are removed
Workflow editor: no-trigger workflow still allows manual run using the existing run dialog
Workflow editor: event-trigger workflow still shows event catalog and mapping controls
Workflow editor: workflow-context link opens the schedules screen filtered to the current workflow
Permissions: users without workflow manage permission cannot create schedules
Permissions: users without workflow manage permission cannot edit, pause, resume, or delete schedules
Tenant isolation: schedule list only returns schedules for the current tenant
DB integration: happy path creates a recurring schedule row with payload_json and runner identifiers in a migrated test database
DB integration: failure path rejects invalid payload_json against the real migrated schedule schema and leaves no row behind
